break_roasts_by_minute: subject has left workstation. logging time. this is fine. breaks are normal. i am not bored. this is a normal surveillance operation.
two minutes. subject still away. i have logged it. i have logged everything. i have been logging things for six hours and nothing has happened. this is fine.
three minutes. i'm going to eat my sandwich now. i wasn't going to eat it until noon but it's three minutes into a break and i need something to do.
four minutes. i have reviewed the footage from this morning. subject typed. subject deleted what they typed. subject stared. that was the morning.
five minutes. checked in with headquarters. they asked for updates. i told them subject took a break. there was a long silence. then they hung up.
six minutes. i have begun ranking the ceiling tiles by interestingness. there are twelve tiles. none of them are interesting. subject has not returned.
seven minutes. i've started a personal log. separate from the official log. it's mostly just 'still nothing' repeated. i think it has literary merit.
eight minutes. i called my contact at the agency. asked if i could be reassigned. they laughed. they actually laughed. subject still not back.
nine minutes. i have memorized the layout of the subject's desk. i could reconstruct it from memory. i have reconstructed it from memory. twice.
ten minutes. ten minutes. i joined this agency to make a difference. to protect something. i am watching an empty chair. the chair is not a threat.
eleven minutes. i've started doing crosswords. the agency doesn't know. i don't care anymore. eleven minutes of an empty workstation.
twelve minutes. i filed a report. it said 'subject on extended break, no suspicious activity, agent morale low'. they flagged the last part as unprofessional.
thirteen minutes. i've been in this van for six hours. i have a thermos of cold coffee and a clear line of sight to someone who is not working. this is my life.
fourteen minutes. i have begun to question the mission. not in a philosophical way. in a very practical 'why am i here specifically' way.
fifteen minutes. fifteen minutes. i once surveilled a target for three weeks. they robbed a bank. exciting. this is not that. this is not anything.
sixteen minutes. i rated this assignment one star in my internal review. no further comments. subject still gone.
seventeen minutes. i've started writing a novel. it's about a surveillance agent who loses their mind watching someone take breaks. it's autobiographical.
eighteen minutes. headquarters called to check on me. i described the break in detail. they told me to 'stay focused'. on what. on the empty chair. focus on the empty chair.
nineteen minutes. i have developed a theory. the subject knows i'm watching. this is psychological warfare. the break is the weapon. i am losing.
twenty minutes. a third of an hour. i have eaten the sandwich. i have drunk the cold coffee. i have nothing left. subject still not at workstation.
twenty one minutes. i submitted a request for backup. they denied it. they said and i quote 'there is nothing happening that requires backup'. correct. nothing is happening. that's the problem.
twenty two minutes. i've started talking to the equipment. the camera hasn't said anything back yet but i feel like we're developing an understanding.
twenty three minutes. i looked up the subject's file again. qualifications. history. potential threat level. the threat level is listed as 'negligible'. i now understand why.
twenty four minutes. i have been awake for nineteen hours. i have been watching this workstation for six of them. the workstation has been empty for twenty four minutes. i need a transfer.
twenty five minutes. twenty five minutes. i once tracked a target across four countries in two days. that was meaningful. this is a chair. i am watching a chair.
twenty six minutes. i filed an emergency psychological evaluation request for myself. they said i seemed fine. i am not fine. subject still not back.
twenty seven minutes. i've named the chair. i'm not going to say what i named it. it doesn't matter. subject still absent. gerald is still empty.
twenty eight minutes. i checked the perimeter. the perimeter is fine. everything is fine. nothing is happening. nothing has happened. nothing will happen. i'm going to eat the backup sandwich.
twenty nine minutes. i'm on the backup sandwich. i didn't think i'd need the backup sandwich. i needed the backup sandwich.
thirty minutes. half an hour. i have filed seven reports. eaten two sandwiches. named a chair. written four chapters of a novel. subject has not worked. i have not slept. we are both failing.
thirty one minutes. headquarters asked for a status update. i sent them a photo of gerald. they did not respond. i think they understand now.
thirty two minutes. i've developed a theory that time moves differently near an empty workstation. thirty two minutes has felt like a career.
thirty three minutes. i requested early retirement today. they said i was too young. i said i felt very old. they said that wasn't a valid retirement reason. it should be.
thirty four minutes. i'm going to write a memoir. 'thirty four minutes: a surveillance agent's account of watching someone not work'. it will be a short book. nothing happened.
thirty five minutes. thirty five minutes. i was top of my class. i speak four languages. i have crossed international borders in the dark with nothing but a compass. and i am watching gerald.
interrupt_roasts: subject leaving workstation. logging it. this is what i do. i log things. it's fine.
subject on break. notifying headquarters. headquarters did not care. logging that too.
movement detected. subject abandoning post. i've seen more action from the parking lot camera.
subject departing. i'm going to eat my sandwich early. this is your fault.
break initiated. i have been waiting for something to happen. this is not what i meant.
subject leaving. i've already written the report. it took four seconds. that's how little is happening.
logging break. current threat level: zero. current morale level: also zero. coincidence.
subject on the move. the move is away from the keyboard. i'm going to do a crossword.
break detected. i've alerted no one because there is nothing to alert anyone about.
subject stepping away. i'm going to name the chair while you're gone.
logging departure. six hours of surveillance and the most exciting thing is this break. the bar is underground.
subject gone. i'm going to talk to the camera for a while. don't rush back.
resume_roasts: subject returning to workstation. logging it. trying to feel something about it. struggling.
subject back. updating report. report now says 'subject returned'. riveting.
there they are. i've told gerald you were coming back. gerald is relieved. i named the chair.
subject has returned. i've informed headquarters. they said 'good'. that's all. just 'good'.
back at the workstation. i'm going to observe very carefully now. out of habit mostly.
subject returned. i've eaten both sandwiches. this is what the break did to me. i hope you're happy.
logging return. current excitement level: minimal. current sandwich count: zero. these are related.
subject back. i've closed the novel. i was on chapter six. it was getting good. you interrupted the novel.
there you are. gerald has been keeping your seat warm. gerald is a chair. i've been here a long time.
subject returned to workstation. i'm filing a report. the report will say you came back. that's the whole report.
back. okay. i'm going to observe you work now. please actually work. i need something to put in the log.
subject at workstation. i'm alert. i'm ready. i've been ready for hours. please do something reportable.
you're back. i've updated headquarters. they asked if anything of note happened. i said no. they said okay. that was our whole conversation.
back at the desk. i have re-aimed the camera. i have adjusted the focus. i am ready to observe. please be worth observing.
subject returned. i put down the crossword. i closed the novel. i said goodbye to gerald. i am fully professional again. please work.
finish_lines: subject completed work session. logging it. this is the most i've had to write all day. it's one sentence.
session complete. i'm filing the final report now. it's mostly about the sandwiches and gerald.
work session finished. i'm calling headquarters. they're going to ask what happened. i'm going to say 'they worked'. that's it. that's the debrief.
subject finished. i've been here for hours for this moment. it's fine. it's a moment. i've logged it.
session complete. i requested a commendation for patience. they said commendations are for field work. this was field work. nobody will ever know.
finished. i'm packing up the van. i'm leaving gerald behind. i don't want to talk about it.
work session over. total incidents: zero. total sandwiches: two. total chairs named: one. mission status: technically complete.
subject completed session. i've submitted my full report. it is four hundred pages. three hundred and ninety are about the break.
done. i'm going home. i'm going to sleep. i'm going to think about gerald. this is the job.
session complete. somewhere out there a threat exists that i could be tracking. instead i watched you work. you're welcome. i think.
quit_line: subject terminating session early. logging it. i've logged everything. every minute. every break. every nothing. i'm going to close the file now. drive back to headquarters. hand in my report. it will say 'nothing happened' in four hundred different ways. goodnight subject. goodnight gerald.
